title: Phase 1: Config module and YAML file
description: Create src/codex_orchestrator/config.py with three frozen dataclasses (SchedulerConfig, BackendConfig, OrchestratorConfig), load_config() and default_config() public functions. Create .orchestrator/config.yaml with default values matching all current hardcoded constants. Add pyyaml>=6,<7 dependency to pyproject.toml.
acceptance_criteria: config.py defines SchedulerConfig, BackendConfig, and OrchestratorConfig as frozen dataclasses
OrchestratorConfig.backend(name) raises KeyError listing valid backends if not found
OrchestratorConfig.allowed_tools_for(backend, agent_type) returns deduplicated union of default + agent-specific tools
load_config(root) falls back to default_config() when file does not exist
default_config() returns values identical to current hardcoded constants
.orchestrator/config.yaml contains all sections from spec (common, codex, claude)

handoff_summary.completed: All acceptance criteria met: frozen dataclasses, KeyError on unknown backend, deduplicated allowed_tools_for(), YAML loading with fallback to defaults, config.yaml with all spec sections, pyyaml dependency added.
handoff_summary.remaining: Phase 2 wiring of config into runner/CLI/scheduler/skills/prompts modules (out of scope for this bead).
handoff_summary.risks: No risks identified. Implementation is additive and does not modify existing module behavior.
handoff_summary.next_action: Proceed to Phase 2: wire config into existing modules (runner.py, cli.py, scheduler.py, skills.py, prompts.py).
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Phase 2 beads that modify runner.py, cli.py, scheduler.py, skills.py, or prompts.py will need to import from config.py and should depend on this bead.

conflict_risks: Phase 2 beads that modify runner.py, cli.py, scheduler.py, skills.py, or prompts.py will need to import from config.py and should depend on this bead.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run targeted validation needed to confirm the implementation.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
